sections.content:

| **Step 06 — Market Needs (JTBD)** | `JTBD_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md` | 26 error statements (E1–E26) |

| **Step 03 — Incumbent Technologies** | `JTBD_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md` (§2.4 Alternatives) | 5 alternatives: Electromagnetic (~30%), Coriolis (~25%), Turbine/Impeller (~8%), No measurement |
| **Step 09 — Feature-Market Fit** | `FIT_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md` | 7 tech features, avg overall_fit = 5.08 |

---

sections.title: Step 1 — Error Statement Collection
sections.content:
26 market-level needs from Step 06 (preserve all fields) + 12 product-constraint needs from Step 10 (injected with `job_step_number=0`, `job_step_name="product_constraint"`, `impact_category="reliability"`, `product_related=true`).

**Total needs for scoring: 38**

---

sections.title: Step 2 — Satisfaction Recalibration (Incumbent Evidence)
sections.content:
Incumbent technology profiles used for recalibration:

| Technology | Share | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|
| Electromagnetic | ~30% | Accuracy ±0.5%, no moving parts, no pressure drop, conductive liquids | No glycol/DI water (>5 μS/cm), no temp co-measurement, 5–20 W power, 2–3× cost |
| Coriolis | ~25% | Highest accuracy ±0.1%, mass flow + density, all liquid media | 5–10× cost, 5–50 kg weight, inherent pressure drop, overkill for volumetric circuits |
| Turbine/Impeller | ~8% | Lowest cost ($5–20 OEM), simple pulse output | Bearing wear 2–3 yr, glycol intolerance, calibration drift, pressure drop, no bidirectional |

**Recalibration result:** Original satisfaction scores from Step 06 were derived with web-researched incumbent awareness across all 6 segments (continuous thermal, batch process, CIP/SIP, safety interlock, fiscal/billing, water infrastructure). Cross-checking against incumbent profiles confirms scores are well-calibrated:

- E11 (dry-run detection, sat=4): Confirmed — turbine sensors cannot detect pipe-empty; electromagnetic detects loss of signal but no advance warning; satisfaction reflects mixed capability across incumbents.
- E9 (calibration drift, sat=3): Confirmed — electromagnetic and Coriolis maintain calibration (55% of market), but turbine drifts significantly and "no measurement" has no baseline; the low satisfaction reflects that UNDETECTED drift remains a problem even with solid-state technologies (no self-verification standard).
- E6 (simultaneous flow+temp, sat=3): Confirmed — electromagnetic, Coriolis, and turbine all require separate temperature probes; only ultrasonic offers integrated temperature in the same sensor body.
- PR1 (acoustic impedance mismatch, sat=3): Confirmed — this is inherent to ultrasonic technology; electromagnetic and Coriolis are unaffected.

**All 38 scores retained as recalibrated values.**

---

sections.title: Step 3 — Opportunity Scores (Deterministic)
sections.content:
**Formula:** `opportunity_score = importance + max(importance - satisfaction, 0)`
**Underserved:** `importance > 7 AND satisfaction < 5`
**Overserved:** `importance < 3 AND satisfaction > 7`

### Step 06 Needs (26 from JTBD)

| ID | Statement (abbreviated) | Step | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Over | product_related |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| E11 | Pipe-empty/dry-run detection speed | MONITOR | 9 | 4 | **14.00** | true | false | false |
| E1 | Flow measurement error >±2% across range | EXECUTE | 9 | 5 | **13.00** | false | false | false |
| E9 | Undetected calibration drift >±0.5%/12 mo | MONITOR | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | false | false |
| E21 | Latent installation fault passing commissioning | CONFIRM | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | true | false | false |
| E22 | OEM qualification time sample→PPAP | P1-Acq | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | true | false | false |
| E23 | Annual maintenance cost per measurement point | P1-Maint | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | true | false | false |
| E24 | Unplanned replacement from mechanical wear <5 yr | P1-Maint | 8 | 4 | **12.00** | true | false | true |
| E3 | Signal loss when air >2% vol >30 s | EXECUTE | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | false | true |
| E5 | Detect reverse-flow >0.5 l/min in <2 s | EXECUTE | 8 | 5 | **11.00** | false | false | false |
| E6 | Simultaneous flow + temp ±0.5°C | EXECUTE | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | false | true |
| E8 | Flow degradation with glycol 0–50% | EXECUTE | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | false | true |
| E12 | Undetected transducer fouling below ±3% | MONITOR | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | false | true |
| E15 | Installation error from insufficient straight run | PREPARE | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | false | true |
| E2 | Repeatability >±0.3% | EXECUTE | 8 | 6 | **10.00** | false | false | false |
| E4 | Accuracy during >20°C temp transient | EXECUTE | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | false | true |
| E10 | Distinguish process deviation from sensor artifact | MONITOR | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | false | false |
| E17 | Incorrect fluid-type configuration error | PREPARE | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | false | true |
| E19 | Sensor range misses ±20% of expected flow | DEFINE | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | false | false |
| E20 | Confirm valid operation <5 min after install | CONFIRM | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | false | false |
| E25 | Verifiable measurement traceability for audits | P3-Role | 7 | 5 | **9.00** | false | false | false |
| E13 | Continuous self-diagnostic health status | MONITOR | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | false | false | false |
| E7 | Pressure drop >50 mbar at nominal flow | EXECUTE | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | false | true |
| E14 | Install + commission time to first measurement | PREPARE | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | false | false |
| E16 | Field config steps before specified accuracy | PREPARE | 6 | 6 | **6.00** | false | false | false |
| E26 | Anxiety from deploying new sensor technology | P5-Emotion | 5 | 4 | **6.00** | false | false | false |
| E18 | Difficulty specifying correct sensor variant | DEFINE | 5 | 6 | **5.00** | false | false | false |

### Step 10 Needs (12 product-constraint needs)

| ID | Statement (abbreviated) | Source Constraint | Imp | Sat | Opp | Under | Over | product_related |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| PR1 | Acoustic impedance mismatch excludes non-aqueous media | Aqueous Media Requirement | 8 | 3 | **13.00** | true | false | true |
| PR3 | Air >2% vol exceeds transit-time compensation | Air Bubble / Void Fraction | 7 | 3 | **11.00** | false | false | true |
| PR7 | Flow dead zone below min threshold (0.25–4.2 l/min) | Min Flow Rate Detection | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | false | true |
| PR9 | 5–10× DN straight-pipe footprint penalty | Min Straight Pipe Run | 7 | 4 | **10.00** | false | false | true |
| PR6 | Measurement outage during partially filled pipe | Pipe Full Condition | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | false | false | true |
| PR10 | 3,000 m³ life endurance consumed in months | Life Endurance Ceiling | 6 | 4 | **8.00** | false | false | true |
| PR2 | Polymer housing/seal degradation at 90°C | Max Medium Temperature | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | false | true |
| PR4 | NTC compensation lag during rapid temp transients | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | 6 | 5 | **7.00** | false | false | true |
| PR5 | CIP chemical attack on wetted polymer | Wetted Material Compatibility | 5 | 5 | **5.00** | false | false | true |
| PR8 | Housing failure from pressure spikes >10 bar | Max Operating Pressure | 5 | 6 | **5.00** | false | false | true |
| PR11 | No ATEX/IECEx excludes hazardous areas | No Explosive Atmosphere Cert | 4 | 3 | **5.00** | false | false | true |
| PR12 | Electronics/connector failure in extreme ambient | Ambient Temperature Range | 4 | 5 | **4.00** | false | false | true |

---

sections.title: Step 4 — Market ODI Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Market** | Flowmeters (Home Industry) |
| **NAICS** | 334513 |
| **Total Needs Scored** | 38 |
| **Underserved Count** | 7 |
| **Overserved Count** | 0 |
| **Avg Opportunity Score** | 9.29 |

### Zone Distribution

| Zone | Score Range | Count | % |
|---|---|---|---|
| Highly Underserved | > 15 | 0 | 0% |
| Underserved | 12–15 | 8 | 21% |
| Appropriately Served | 10–12 | 13 | 34% |
| Overserved | < 10 | 17 | 45% |

### Top 5 Opportunities

| Rank | ID | Statement | Opp | Imp | Sat | Zone |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | E11 | Minimize the time to detect pipe-empty or dry-run conditions that could damage downstream equipment | **14.00** | 9 | 4 | Underserved |
| 2 | E1 | Minimize the likelihood of flow measurement error exceeding ±2% of reading across the 10–100% flow range | **13.00** | 9 | 5 | Underserved |
| 3 | E9 | Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±0.5% over 12 months of continuous operation | **13.00** | 8 | 3 | Underserved |
| 4 | PR1 | Minimize the likelihood of measurement failure when target process fluids have acoustic impedance outside the piezoelectric transducer's coupling range | **13.00** | 8 | 3 | Underserved |
| 5 | E21 | Minimize the likelihood of passing commissioning verification when the sensor has a latent installation fault | **12.00** | 8 | 4 | Underserved |

*Tiebreaker: importance descending, then satisfaction ascending, then market-level before product-related.*

---

sections.title: Step 5 — Cross-Check vs Feature-Market Fit (Step 09)
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| ODI avg opportunity score | 9.29 |
| Feature-Fit avg (tech features) | 5.08 |

**Cross-check thresholds:**

| Condition | Threshold | Actual | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| High opportunity, low feature fit | ODI avg > 12 AND Fit avg < 4 | 9.29, 5.08 | **No flag** |
| Low opportunity, high feature fit | ODI avg < 8 AND Fit avg > 7 | 9.29, 5.08 | **No flag** |

**No inconsistencies detected.** ODI average (9.29) indicates a mature market with moderate unmet needs — consistent with a home industry where the product is already deployed. Feature-Fit average (5.08) indicates solid technology alignment. The combination is coherent: the sensor addresses the strongest needs (dry-run detection, drift, reliability) while product-constraint needs (acoustic impedance, air bubbles) limit addressable scope.

---

sections.title: Key Findings
sections.content:
| Insight | Detail |
|---|---|
| **#1 opportunity** | Pipe-empty/dry-run detection (E11, Opp=14.0) — highest-scoring need across the entire matrix; Digital Anomaly Detection feature directly addresses it |
| **Reliability cluster** | 5 of 8 underserved needs (E9, E21, E22, E23, E24) relate to measurement reliability, commissioning quality, and maintenance burden — ultrasonic technology's no-moving-parts advantage directly targets this cluster |
| **Product-constraint risk** | PR1 (acoustic impedance, Opp=13.0) is the only product-related need in the top 5 — the aqueous media constraint is the single biggest limitation on addressable market scope |
| **Incumbent vulnerability** | Turbine/impeller (8% share) drives 3 underserved needs: E24 (mechanical wear), E23 (maintenance cost), E9 (calibration drift) — all eliminated by solid-state ultrasonic technology |
| **Co-measurement gap** | E6 (flow+temp, Opp=11.0) — no competing technology provides integrated temperature; this is a unique differentiator addressing thermal energy calculation needs across all 6 segments |
| **No overserved needs** | Zero overserved entries; no incumbent over-delivers on any dimension — market is uniformly opportunity-rich |
| **Mature home market** | 45% of needs score <10 (appropriately served or below), reflecting that the home industry is well-served overall; differentiation must focus on the 21% underserved needs |

---

sections.title: QA Checklist
sections.content:
| # | Check | Pass Condition | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 | Formula correct | Spot-check 5 entries match `opp = imp + max(imp - sat, 0)` | **PASS** — E11: 9+max(5,0)=14; E9: 8+max(5,0)=13; PR8: 5+max(-1,0)=5; E16: 6+max(0,0)=6; PR12: 4+max(-1,0)=4 |
| Q2 | Underserved flags correct | All imp>7 AND sat<5 flagged, no others | **PASS** — 7 entries flagged; verified E1(imp=9,sat=5)=false, E9(imp=8,sat=3)=true, E5(imp=8,sat=5)=false |
| Q3 | Overserved flags correct | All imp<3 AND sat>7 flagged, no others | **PASS** — 0 entries; min importance=4, max satisfaction=6 |
| Q4 | Need count preserved | Total ≥ Step 06 count (26) | **PASS** — 38 (26 + 12 from Step 10) |
| Q5 | Top-5 ordering correct | Sorted by descending opp score | **PASS** — 14.0, 13.0, 13.0, 13.0, 12.0 with importance tiebreaker |
| Q6 | Cross-check ran | ODI-Feature-Fit inconsistencies logged | **PASS** — no inconsistencies; both thresholds checked |

---

sections.content:
- [JTBD_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md](JTBD_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md) — 26 error statements, incumbent technology anchors, job map
- [FIT_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md](FIT_flowmeters_src_01_product_decomposition_.md) — 12 product-related needs (Step 10), 7 tech feature-market fit scores (Step 09)
- [01_product_decomposition.md](01_product_decomposition.md) — product features, constraints, specifications
- [02_functional_promise.md](02_functional_promise.md) — commodity FP, product FP, differentiators
- [05_market_discovery.md](05_market_discovery.md) — market discovery and architecture distance scoring

rows.Count: 5 alternatives: Electromagnetic (~30%), Coriolis (~25%), Turbine/Impeller (~8%), No measurement

rows.Strengths: Accuracy ±0.5%, no moving parts, no pressure drop, conductive liquids
rows.Weaknesses: No glycol/DI water (>5 μS/cm), no temp co-measurement, 5–20 W power, 2–3× cost

rows.Strengths: Highest accuracy ±0.1%, mass flow + density, all liquid media
rows.Weaknesses: 5–10× cost, 5–50 kg weight, inherent pressure drop, overkill for volumetric circuits

rows.Strengths: Lowest cost ($5–20 OEM), simple pulse output
rows.Weaknesses: Bearing wear 2–3 yr, glycol intolerance, calibration drift, pressure drop, no bidirectional

rows.Statement (abbreviated): Annual maintenance cost per measurement point
Unplanned replacement from mechanical wear <5 yr
Signal loss when air >2% vol >30 s
Installation error from insufficient straight run
Distinguish process deviation from sensor artifact
Sensor range misses ±20% of expected flow
Confirm valid operation <5 min after install
Pressure drop >50 mbar at nominal flow
Install + commission time to first measurement
Field config steps before specified accuracy
Anxiety from deploying new sensor technology
Acoustic impedance mismatch excludes non-aqueous media
Flow dead zone below min threshold (0.25–4.2 l/min)
Measurement outage during partially filled pipe
3,000 m³ life endurance consumed in months
NTC compensation lag during rapid temp transients
CIP chemical attack on wetted polymer
Housing failure from pressure spikes >10 bar

rows.Statement: Minimize the time to detect pipe-empty or dry-run conditions that could damage downstream equipment
Minimize the likelihood of flow measurement error exceeding ±2% of reading across the 10–100% flow range
Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±0.5% over 12 months of continuous operation
Minimize the likelihood of measurement failure when target process fluids have acoustic impedance outside the piezoelectric transducer's coupling range
Minimize the likelihood of passing commissioning verification when the sensor has a latent installation fault

rows.Detail: Pipe-empty/dry-run detection (E11, Opp=14.0) — highest-scoring need across the entire matrix; Digital Anomaly Detection feature directly addresses it

5 of 8 underserved needs (E9, E21, E22, E23, E24) relate to measurement reliability, commissioning quality, and maintenance burden — ultrasonic technology's no-moving-parts advantage directly targets this cluster
PR1 (acoustic impedance, Opp=13.0) is the only product-related need in the top 5 — the aqueous media constraint is the single biggest limitation on addressable market scope
Turbine/impeller (8% share) drives 3 underserved needs: E24 (mechanical wear), E23 (maintenance cost), E9 (calibration drift) — all eliminated by solid-state ultrasonic technology
E6 (flow+temp, Opp=11.0) — no competing technology provides integrated temperature; this is a unique differentiator addressing thermal energy calculation needs across all 6 segments
Zero overserved entries; no incumbent over-delivers on any dimension — market is uniformly opportunity-rich
45% of needs score <10 (appropriately served or below), reflecting that the home industry is well-served overall; differentiation must focus on the 21% underserved needs

rows.Pass Condition: Spot-check 5 entries match `opp = imp + max(imp - sat, 0)`
All imp>7 AND sat<5 flagged, no others
rows.Result: **PASS** — 7 entries flagged; verified E1(imp=9,sat=5)=false, E9(imp=8,sat=3)=true, E5(imp=8,sat=5)=false

rows.Pass Condition: All imp<3 AND sat>7 flagged, no others

entities.naics_title: Instruments and Related Products Manufacturing for Measuring, Displaying, Indicating, Recording, Transmitting, and Controlling Industrial Process Variables

top_opportunities: Minimize the time to detect pipe-empty or dry-run conditions that could damage downstream equipment
Minimize the likelihood of flow measurement error exceeding ±2% of reading across the 10-100% flow range
Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±0.5% over 12 months of continuous operation
Minimize the likelihood of measurement failure when target process fluids have acoustic impedance outside the piezoelectric transducer's coupling range
Minimize the likelihood of passing commissioning verification when the sensor has a latent installation fault

entries.error_statement: Minimize the time to detect pipe-empty or dry-run conditions that could damage downstream equipment
Minimize the likelihood of flow measurement error exceeding ±2% of reading across the 10-100% flow range
Minimize the likelihood of undetected calibration drift exceeding ±0.5% over 12 months of continuous operation

Minimize the likelihood of measurement failure when target process fluids have acoustic impedance outside the piezoelectric transducer's coupling range, excluding non-aqueous media from the addressable application space
Minimize the likelihood of passing commissioning verification when the sensor has a latent installation fault (bond-line defect, misaligned transducer)
Minimize the time required to qualify a new flow sensor for OEM production from initial sample to PPAP approval
Minimize the annual maintenance cost per flow measurement point over a 10-year operational lifecycle
Minimize the likelihood of unplanned sensor replacement due to mechanical wear or seal degradation within 5 years of installation
Minimize the likelihood of measurement signal loss when entrained air exceeds 2% by volume for >30 seconds
Increase the ability to detect reverse-flow events exceeding 0.5 l/min within <2 seconds
Increase the ability to simultaneously measure fluid temperature within ±0.5°C alongside flow rate at a single measurement point
Minimize the likelihood of flow measurement degradation when glycol concentration changes between 0% and 50% by volume
Minimize the likelihood of undetected transducer fouling or degradation reducing measurement accuracy below ±3%
Minimize the likelihood of installation-induced measurement error due to insufficient upstream straight pipe run (<5x DN)
Minimize the measurement error caused by entrained air exceeding 2% by volume, which attenuates the ultrasonic signal beyond the transit-time algorithm's compensation capability
Increase the ability to maintain flow reading repeatability within ±0.3% under identical process conditions across consecutive measurement windows
Increase the ability to sustain specified accuracy when fluid temperature changes by >20°C within 60 seconds
Increase the ability to distinguish genuine process deviations from sensor artifacts with >95% classification accuracy
Minimize the likelihood of measurement error caused by incorrect fluid-type configuration at commissioning
Minimize the flow measurement dead zone below the minimum detectable threshold (0.25-4.2 l/min depending on DN) where transit-time signal-to-noise ratio prevents valid measurement
Minimize the installation footprint penalty from the 5-10x DN straight-pipe requirement that restricts deployment in space-constrained process skids, compact equipment, and retrofit installations
Minimize the likelihood of specifying a sensor whose operating range does not cover ±20% beyond the expected process flow range
Increase the ability to confirm valid sensor operation within <5 minutes of installation completion
Increase the ability to demonstrate verifiable measurement traceability and calibration compliance during external quality or energy audits
Increase the ability to provide continuous self-diagnostic health status to the host controller via standard communication protocols
Minimize the duration of measurement outage during system events that temporarily create partially filled pipe conditions where the ultrasonic pulse cannot traverse the air gap
Minimize the frequency of sensor replacement in continuous high-throughput applications where the 3,000 m3 total throughput endurance is consumed within months rather than years of operation
Minimize the pressure drop across the measurement point to <50 mbar at nominal flow rate
Minimize the time required to install and commission a flow sensor from unpacking to first valid measurement
Minimize the likelihood of polymer housing and seal degradation when process circuits operate at sustained temperatures approaching the 90 degrees C material service limit
Minimize the residual transit-time measurement error during rapid temperature transients where speed-of-sound changes outpace the NTC compensation algorithm's response rate
Minimize the number of field configuration steps required before the sensor delivers specified accuracy
Minimize the anxiety associated with deploying a new sensor technology by providing continuous self-diagnostic status confirmation
Increase the ease of specifying the correct sensor variant for a given pipe diameter, medium, and flow range combination
Minimize the likelihood of wetted polymer housing and seal attack when process fluids or CIP chemicals exceed the material's chemical resistance limits
Minimize the risk of housing structural failure in applications where process pressure spikes or water hammer events temporarily exceed the 10 bar continuous rating
Minimize the exclusion of hazardous-area applications where the absence of ATEX/IECEx certification prevents sensor deployment in Zone 1/0 explosive atmosphere classifications
Minimize the risk of electronics failure or RAST 2.5 connector degradation in outdoor or extreme-ambient installations that exceed the sensor's indoor-rated operating envelope
